Use an array to store all state values and an index (cursor) to track which useState call you're on.
Reset the index to 0 at the start of each render. Each useState call reads states[index] and increments the index.
On first render, initialize the state at the current index. On subsequent renders, use the existing stored value.
The setState function must capture the current index via closure, and support both direct values and updater functions: typeof newValue === 'function' ? newValue(prev) : newValue.
